taxonomy_v3: Engineering and Development, Cyber Security, Security Engineer
core_skills: Cloud platforms, Microsoft Azure, Amazon Web Services, Cloud security engineering, DevSecOps
skill_match_scores: Cloud platforms:1.0, Microsoft Azure:0.0, Amazon Web Services:1.0, Cloud security engineering:0.0, DevSecOps:0.0
must_have_quals: Hands‑on experience with any major cloud platform (Azure preferred; AWS, GCP, or OCI are a plus), Experience engineering or operating cloud tools, platforms, or automation solutions, Familiarity with security concepts in at least one of the following areas: IAM, vulnerability management, data protection, logging/monitoring, network controls, or encryption, Experience with CI/CD pipelines, cloud provisioning, and automation tools, Understanding of containers, serverless technologies, or orchestration platforms, Proficiency in scripting, programming, or coding (Bash, Python, PowerShell, Java, etc.), Strong interpersonal, collaboration, consulting, and documentation skills, Demonstrated ability to build, refine, and mature processes and metrics
preferred_quals: Familiarity with security or compliance frameworks (NIST, CIS Benchmarks, PCI DSS), Experience in architecture, engineering, operations, compliance, risk management, or audit functions, Understanding of defensive technologies (IPS, WAF, HIPS, AV, etc.), Knowledge of network and system attack vectors and corresponding mitigation approaches, Experience with log analysis platforms such as Splunk, Prior experience supporting audits or remediating audit findings, Technical experience in at least one foundational discipline: networking, servers, or endpoint engineering, Understanding of how to evaluate, adopt, and responsibly apply AI driven tools
core_responsibilities: Promote DevSecOps culture including training / outreach to application development teams with special focus on security controls (IAM, security compliance), Design, build, and/or deploy tools and reports that provide visibility into cloud accounts, instances, and services, Implement infrastructure-as-code and CI/CD automation to standardize and scale cloud security team deployments, Stay current on cloud security vulnerabilities, policies, standards, regulations to ensure cloud environments are managing risk appropriately, Continuously work with business partners on vulnerability remediation strategies, Partner with security teams to enhance cloud security solutions and establish preventative controls to support security needs via automation, Partner with security teams to ensure migrated workloads maintain compliance and governance requirements, Collaborate with Information Risk Management to ensure all cloud products / services are meeting configuration and compliance standards, Implement and support data protection and encryption strategies for cloud services, products, and data, Implement and support best practice requirements for logging and monitoring
job_summary: Nationwide is a Fortune 100 company focused on delivering extraordinary care to its customers. The Cloud Security Engineering team is seeking a hands-on technologist to implement and mature cloud controls, ensuring security and compliance across cloud platforms while promoting a DevSecOps culture.
